description: Baseline: unmodified s3dashmap. 25.4M ops/sec, 84.9% hit rate, CV 17.4% (noisy)

asi.note: CV=17.4% is noisy — need large improvements to be confident. ~25-28M ops/sec range across runs. 12 cores = 48 shards (default), shard_cap=625 per shard for 30K items.

description: Replace bump_freq CAS loop with simple load+store. +7.8% throughput, CV 3%

asi.hypothesis: CAS loop in bump_freq (80% of ops) replaced with relaxed load+store. Races harmless since freq is a heuristic saturating at 3.
asi.insight: fetch_update CAS loop was contended on hot entries under concurrent reads

description: Ghost set stores hashes instead of full keys — eliminates key cloning. +0.5% throughput, p99 improved 1.67→1.46µs

asi.hypothesis: Replace ghost HashSet<K> with HashSet<u64> to eliminate key cloning on eviction path
asi.result: marginal throughput gain but nice p99 improvement and code simplification
asi.insight: ghost set is only touched on write path eviction (~20% of ops), so savings are limited. The real win is less memory pressure from not cloning string keys.

description: Eliminate double map.find() in eviction + simplify freq decrement. No throughput gain (-0.75%)

asi.hypothesis: Eliminate redundant map.find() in evict_from_small (was called twice for same key) and replace CAS freq decrement with simple store under write lock
asi.rollback_reason: primary metric within noise of previous best
asi.next_action_hint: eviction path optimizations have diminishing returns on 80/20 read workload. Focus on read path or structural changes (shard count, hash function)
asi.insight: double find was already fast due to CPU cache locality — second find hits warm cache line

description: New baseline for eff_ops_sec focus. 23.1M eff ops/s, p50=0.12µs, p99=1.50µs, tail=1.38µs

asi.hypothesis: baseline with bump_freq + ghost hash optimizations already applied
asi.focus: effective throughput (ops_sec * hit_rate), p50, p99, tail latency
asi.note: hit_rate=84.9% is stable; improving it would multiply into eff_ops_sec. p99=1.5µs, tail=1.38µs are the latency targets.

description: Pre-allocate hash table + queues to shard capacity, inline hot path functions. p99: 1.50→1.46µs, tail: 1.38→1.33µs

asi.hypothesis: Pre-allocate map to shard_cap, VecDeques to 2x capacity, HashSet to ghost_cap. Add #[inline] to hash_u64, determine_shard, evict_one
asi.result: consistent p99 reduction 1.50→1.46µs and tail 1.38→1.33µs across 2 runs. eff_ops modest +0.7-3.5%
asi.insight: pre-allocation eliminates resize spikes that cause tail latency. Inline hints help LTO but marginal in release profile

description: Eliminate double map.find() in eviction, replace CAS with store under write lock. +1.7% eff_ops, p99/tail maintained

asi.hypothesis: Single find() per eviction candidate instead of two; replace fetch_update CAS with simple store for freq decrement (safe under exclusive write lock)
asi.result: +1.7% eff_ops. p99=1.46 and tail=1.33 unchanged from pre-alloc improvement
asi.insight: eviction path improvements compound: pre-alloc removed resize spikes, single-find reduces per-eviction cost

description: Direct _insert bypasses Entry API: fast update-in-place for occupied, inline eviction for vacant. p99: 1.46→1.38µs, tail: 1.33→1.25µs, CV: 1.0%

asi.hypothesis: Direct _insert without Entry enum dispatch. Occupied path: single find_or_find_insert_slot + in-place value swap. Vacant path: inline ghost check + eviction + insert.
asi.result: p99 1.46→1.38µs, tail 1.33→1.25µs, CV dropped to 1.0%. eff_ops +0.4%
asi.insight: eliminating Entry enum construction + match dispatch reduces instructions on write path. The consistency improvement (CV 1.0%) suggests less variance in write latency
asi.key_win: p99 and tail latency significantly reduced

description: Reduce shard multiplier 4x→2x (64→32 shards). REGRESSION: -10% eff_ops, p99: 1.38→1.79µs

asi.hypothesis: Fewer shards (32 vs 64) = larger per-shard tables, less overhead, but more lock contention
asi.rollback_reason: significant regression in all metrics. 32 shards on 12 cores creates too much contention
asi.next_action_hint: 64 shards is correct for 12 cores. Try MORE shards or different structural changes. Do NOT reduce shard count.
asi.insight: shard_count=4*ncpu is well-tuned. contention dominates at 2*ncpu for this workload.

description: Add #[inline] to _get, _yield_read_shard, _yield_write_shard. No consistent improvement — LTO already inlines

asi.hypothesis: #[inline] on hot path trait methods to help compiler
asi.rollback_reason: no consistent improvement. first run showed good p99 but second run reverted. LTO in release profile already inlines monomorphized trait methods
asi.next_action_hint: inline hints are useless with LTO. focus on algorithmic/structural changes: reduce key cloning, improve eviction cache-friendliness, or reduce lock hold time
